response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack web engineer with deep TypeScript/Node.js expertise, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. His C++ experience is minimal — limited to roughly six months at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project — far short of the mandatory 5–10 years. He has no demonstrable fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge, and no evidence of Windows-platform development, SQL Server, or Oracle experience. His one tangentially relevant role (RIDE Capital) was a fintech startup focused on retail investment flows, not institutional capital markets systems.
strengths: Solid software craftsmanship fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development practiced consistently across multiple roles
Brief C++ and C exposure at National Instruments, indicating at least some foundational familiarity with the language
Java experience across multiple roles satisfies one minor desirable criterion
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months) against a mandatory requirement of 5–10 years; primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js
Zero fixed income, derivatives, or institutional capital markets domain knowledge present in the resume
No Windows-platform, SQL Server, or Oracle experience evident; profile is Linux/web-centric throughout
